  • Appendix
  • Templates
  • Student
  • Student Template

Student Template

(226 Fields, 70 Collected, Delimited)

Target Table(s): STUDENT

Load Sequence/Dependency: No dependencies

Template Description​

The Student Template contains one LEA record for each student for each school year. The Student Template can be submitted at all reporting periods other than end of year in either a point-in-time fashion (only including active students) or in a cumulative fashion (all students enrolled in the LEA for the school year).

The end of year Student Template submission must be a cumulative submission. All students, regardless of whether or not they were active on a PDE reporting date, must be included in the end of year submission.

note

Not all fields are required at each submission period.

Template Specifications​

1 - SUBMITTING AUN​

RequiredKeyMax Length: 9

The unique, 9-digit Administrative Unit Number (AUN) assigned by PDE for the LEA that is reporting the student.

Sample / Valid Value:

All LEA and School Numbers can be found on the EdNA website.

123456789

eScholar Name: DISTRICT CODE

2 - SCHOOL NUMBER​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 4

The PDE-defined, 4-digit code identifying the school that is reporting the student. All public schools have a 4-digit school code that is other than 0000 or 9999.

NOTES:

  • IUs, PRRIs and APSs use School Number 0000.
  • If a student is at a location that does not have a designated school code, use 9999 as the School Number. PSSA and Keystone precodes will not be generated for code 9999.
Business Rules:
  • For Child Accounting, a student's average daily attendance (ADA) is attributed to the SCHOOL NUMBER populated in this record.
  • For Special Education – the Special Education Reporting LEA uses 9999 for students educated in other LEAs.
Sample / Valid Value:

All LEA and School Numbers can be found on the EdNA website.

1234

eScholar Name: LOCATION CODE

3 - SCHOOL YEAR DATE​

RequiredKeyMax Length: 10

A single date (June 30), the last day of the current school year indicating the whole school year.

Sample / Valid Value:

2024-06-30

4 - PASECUREID​

RequiredKeyMax Length: 10

The unique, 10-digit PAsecureID assigned to the student. Informs various indicators in the Future Ready PA Index.

Business Rules:

STUDENT IDs must be unique within an LEA. Therefore, only a single instance of a student is to be supplied in source file.

Sample / Valid Value:

1000000009

eScholar Name: STUDENT ID

5-9​

Fields not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

10 - GRADE​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 3

Indication of the student's grade level.

Business Rules:

Special Ed students must be coded with an actual grade level. The appropriate grade level can be found on the student's IEP.

Sample / Valid Value:

See Appendix K for a complete list of valid values.

eScholar Name: CURRENT GRADE LEVEL

11-12​

Fields not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

13 - HOME ROOM​

OptionalUpdateableMax Length: 20

Provide the homeroom number for the student for the given year. This field is used for determining class size in the elementary school grades and in off-site preschool programs.

Business Rules:

LEAs with registered Adult Affidavit Programs (AAPs) may code all AAP grade-level students as AAPHR.

Sample / Valid Value:

206A

14 - BIRTH DATE​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 10

Provide the birth date of the student.

Sample / Valid Value:

2015-07-15

15 - GENDER (Federal Reporting)​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

Provide a code that represents the gender of the student. This may be the student's preferred gender. This is for federal reporting.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • M – male
  • F – female

eScholar Name: GENDER CODE

16 - ADDRESS 1​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 40

Provide the street name and number of the student's mailing address.

Business Rules:

Address information is needed for CTE secondary students, Perkins funded CTE adult affidavit program students and Special Education students.

Sample / Valid Value:

222 Main St.

17 - ADDRESS 2​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 40

Provide additional information concerning the street address of the student's mailing address, such as apartment number.

Business Rules:

Address information is needed for CTE secondary students, Perkins funded CTE adult affidavit program students and Special Education students.

Sample / Valid Value:

Apt. 107

18 - CITY​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 30

Provide the city of the student's mailing address.

Business Rules:

Address information is needed for CTE secondary students, Perkins funded CTE adult affidavit program students and Special Education students.

Sample / Valid Value:

York

19 - STATE CODE​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 2

Provide the official 2-character US Postal Service abbreviation for the student's mailing address.

Business Rules:

Address information is needed for CTE secondary students, Perkins funded CTE adult affidavit program students and Special Education students.

Sample / Valid Value:

See Appendix D for the full list of state abbreviations. Example: PA

20 - ZIP​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 10

Provide the official US Postal Code for the student's mailing address.

Business Rules:

If this field is populated, the values should be in the format ###### or #####-####.

Sample / Valid Value:

17401 or 17401-1234

eScholar Name: FULL ZIP CODE

21-26​

Fields not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

27 - RACE / ETHNICITY​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 2

A single code indicating the race and ethnicity of the student. Informs various indicators in the Future Ready PA Index.

Business Rules:

Refer to "Race/Ethnicity: Federal Race and Ethnicity Standards" in this volume of the manual for an explanation of the federal race and ethnicity codes.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • 1 – American Indian/Alaskan Native (not Hispanic)
  • 3 – Black or African American (not Hispanic)
  • 4 – Hispanic (any race)
  • 5 – White (not Hispanic)
  • 6 – Multi-Racial (not Hispanic)
  • 9 – Asian (not Hispanic)
  • 10 – Native Hawaiian or other Pacific Islander (not Hispanic)

eScholar Name: RACE OR ETHNICITY CODE

28-32​

Fields not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

33 - FOOD PGM PARTICIPATION CODE​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

Use this field to indicate if a student participates in free or reduced lunch program.

Business Rules:

LEAs with registered AAPs should code all AAP grade-level students as N.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • F – free
  • R – reduced price
  • N – neither

34 - CHALLENGE TYPE​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 4

Student's primary Challenge Type. This often is referred to as Disability throughout PDE.

note

This MUST match Field 11 PRIMARY DISABILITY of the Special Education Snapshot Template.

Business Rules:

This field is required only if Field 38 SPECIAL EDUCATION = Y or E.

Sample / Valid Value:

See Appendix H for a complete list of valid values.

35-37​

Fields not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

38 - SPECIAL EDUCATION​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

Provide an indication of whether the student is a special education student. This field is used for tracking presence of IEP for Precode. Informs various indicators in the Future Ready PA Index.

Business Rules:

LEAs with registered AAPs should code all AAP grade-level students as N.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • Y – has IEP
  • E – exited IEP and transferred to Regular Ed this school year
  • N – no IEP

39-40​

Fields not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

41 - EL STATUS​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 2

This field indicates whether the student: is currently identified as an EL; Is currently identified as an EL who has Limited or Interrupted Formal Education (LIFE); was formerly EL, has exited, and is being monitored; was formerly EL, has exited, and no longer is being monitored, or; was never identified as EL. Informs various indicators in the Future Ready PA Index.

Business Rules:
  • Code AAPs grade-level students as 01 when student has limited ability in speaking, reading, writing or understanding English language and whose native language is other than English or lives in environment in which a language other than English is dominant. Otherwise, code AAP students as 99.
  • Criteria used to determine if the student has Limited or Interrupted Formal Education (LIFE) Valid Value 06: is enrolling after grade 2, and has a literacy score of less than 3.5 on the WIDA Screener, and has at least two fewer years of age appropriate schooling than peers or has unenrolled from U.S. Schools to enroll in schools in other countries (including Puerto Rico) more than two times in the past four years, and has limited encoding/ decoding skills in native language (as indicated by family interview and/ or native language measures and/or review of academic records)
Sample / Valid Value:
  • 01 – current EL, not LIFE
  • 03 – former EL, exited, and in 1st year of monitoring
  • 04 – former EL, exited, and in 2nd year of monitoring
  • 05 – former EL, exited, and no longer monitored
  • 06 – current EL, LIFE (Limited or Interrupted Formal Education)
  • 07 – former EL, exited, and in 3rd year of monitoring
  • 08 – former EL, exited, and in 4th year of monitoring
  • 99 – never EL

eScholar Name: LEP PARTICIPATION

42-44​

Fields not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

45 - LIEP TYPE (Language Instruction Educational Program)​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 2

LIEP Type provided to EL student (if student is in multiple Programs, select the value of the Program providing the highest percentage of instruction) - LIEP Classifications Guidance

Business Rules:

Select only one program for each individual student. This field is required for all students where Field 41 (EL STATUS) = 01 or 06. A Flow Chart can be found on page 4 of the hyperlink in the Definition.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • 21 - Mixed Bilingual
  • 22 - EL Bilingual
  • 23 - EL Specific Transitional Instruction
  • 24 - Mixed Classes with Native Language Support
  • 25 - EL Specific English-only
  • 26 - Mixed Classes with English-only Support
  • 27 - Parental refusal-mixed classes with English-only support
  • 98 - LIEP type 21-26 provided by another entity – English only support provided at this entity

eScholar Name: SPECIAL PROGRAM CODE

46-49​

Fields not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

50 - BID SCHOOL NUMBER​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 4

The School Number within the LEA that is responsible for the student based on Best Interest Determination. If it is determined to be in the child's best interest, the child has the right to remain in the school of origin. If this occurs, the educating (receiving) entity should populate this field with the school number (4-digit building number) of the Best Interest Determination (BID) school.

Business Rules:

School Number must exist within BID AUN (Field 51).

Sample / Valid Value:
  • 1234

eScholar Name: PREVIOUS LOCATION CODE

51 - BID AUN​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 9

The AUN of the LEA that is responsible for the student based on Best Interest Determination. If it is determined to be in the child's best interest, the child has the right to remain in the school of origin. If this occurs, the educating (receiving) entity should populate this field with the AUN of the Best Interest Determination (BID) school district.

Business Rules:

Will not match Submitting AUN (Field 1)

Sample / Valid Value:
  • 123456789

eScholar Name: PREVIOUS DISTRICT CODE

52-64​

Fields not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

65 - GRAD STATUS​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

Indication of whether a student graduates, drops out, or transfers.

Business Rules:

This field pertains to graduation status and dropout status, as well as transfers. Use appropriate code for students for whom one of the situations in the Valid Values list applies. This field does not apply to AAPs students.

Sample / Valid Value:

See Appendix AL for a complete list of valid values.

eScholar Name: GRADUATION STATUS CODE

66 - EXPECTED POST-GRADUATE ACTIVITY​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 3

Indication of the type of activity the student plans for after graduation or after dropping out.

Business Rules:

Value 998 is valid for dropouts only. Values 010 – 100 are valid for graduates only. Value 997 is valid for special education students with an IEP only. Required for dropouts and high school graduates only.

Sample / Valid Value:

See Appendix M for a complete list of valid values.

67 - STUDENT STATUS​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

Indication of the current status of the student.

Business Rules:

Required for students that fall into one of the situations described in the valid value list.

Sample / Valid Value:

See Appendix L for a complete list of valid values.

68 - DATE FIRST ENROLLED IN AN ELD or BILINGUAL EDUCATION PROGRAM​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 10

The date when the student was initially enrolled in an ELD or bilingual education program in a US school. Use best information available on prior enrollments.

Business Rules:

This field does not apply to AAPs students that are coded as "Current EL" in Field 41 (EL STATUS).

Sample / Valid Value:
  • 2008-09-01

eScholar Name: LAST STATUS DATE

70 - 504 PLAN​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

This field identifies students who: (1) have a current Section 504 service agreement plan in place AND (2) are considered disabled and eligible for protection under the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) of 1990, as amended by the ADA Amendments Act of 2008. ADA (as amended) defines "disability" as (1) a physical or mental impairment that substantially limits a major life activity; (2) a record of such an impairment; or (3) being regarded as having such an impairment.

Business Rules:

Populate this data element, as appropriate, for all students. Consult appropriate LEA Section 504/ADA compliance officer(s) to determine those students with a current Section 504 service agreement plan in place. NOTE: Populate this field with N for students recorded as having a current/active IEP in Field 38 (SPECIAL EDUCATION) of this template.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • Y - Student has a current Section 504 service agreement plan in place and is considered disabled per ADA.
  • N - Student does not have a current Section 504 service agreement plan in place.

eScholar Name: ADA STATUS INDICATOR

73 - FOREIGN EXCHANGE STUDENT​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

An indication of whether the student has entered the country on a student visa (usually 2-year) and is not intending to remain here permanently.

Business Rules:

LEAs with registered AAPs may code all AAP grade-level students as N. Student's age must be 15 or greater.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • Y
  • N

80 - GIFTED​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 2

Indicates the student's gifted status and participation in a Gifted and Talented program.

Business Rules:

LEAs with registered AAPs may code all AAP grade-level students as N.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • GY - Gifted, has GIEP
  • GS - Gifted, receives gifted services through IEP
  • GX - Gifted, does not receive gifted services
  • N - Not applicable

eScholar Name: GIFTED PARTICIPATION CODE

88 - ECONOMIC DISADVANTAGED STATUS CODE​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

This field indicates the economically disadvantaged status of a student and is used to provide the economically disadvantaged disaggregation. Informs various indicators in the Future Ready PA Index.

Business Rules:

To determine if a student is economically disadvantaged, poverty data sources such as Temporary Assistance for Needy Families cases, census poor, Medicaid, children living in institutions for the neglected or delinquent, or those supported in foster homes may be used. If such data are not available, use the most recent reliable data available at the time of determination, such as free and reduced-price lunch eligibility. For Community Eligibility Provision (CEP) schools, do not use free and reduced-price lunch eligibility to determine a student's economic status. By definition, all students in CEP schools are to be reported as food program eligible (Field 131 of the Student and Student Snapshot Templates). In addition to using other poverty source data indicators, LEAs with AAPs CTE enrollees should populate with Y if AAP student is eligible to receive a Pell grant or comparable state need-based financial assistance.

Sample / Valid Value:

  • Y

  • N

89​

Field not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

90 - MOBILITY CODE​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

Identification of specific mobility situations.

Business Rules:

Use C in the case of a Special Education referral student not being educated by your LEA, where the student is referred to an outside placement that does not report to PIMS.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • A - "No Show or Drop Out" - student who was never educated by this LEA in the reporting SY
  • B - "Summer Transfer" - student who was never educated by this LEA in the reporting SY
  • C - "Other" – student who was never educated by this LEA during the reporting SY.
    • Example: Special Education student referred to an outside placement who was never educated by your LEA.

91 - DIPLOMA TYPE CODE​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 3

The type of diploma/credential that is awarded to a student in recognition of their completion of the curricular requirements.

Business Rules:

This field is required for students that have graduated. (Field 65 - GRAD STATUS = G).

Sample / Valid Value:
  • 806 - Regular diploma
  • 816 - General Education Development (GED) credential
  • 826 - Keystone Diploma

92​

Field not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

93 - ALTERNATE STUDENT ID​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 25

A student ID that is different than the primary identifier associated such as a local student identifier.

94​

Field not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

95 - TITLE III EL ELIGIBILITY​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

A Title III Indicator to identify EL students that are Title III served in districts that accept Title III funds. Indicate if the student participates in or benefits from any supplemental activities or receives any supplemental materials funded by Title III. All ELs in a district that accepts Title III funds may not be Title III served.

Business Rules:

This field is conditionally required for ELs with a Valid Value of 01 or 06 in Field 41 (EL STATUS).

Sample / Valid Value:
  • Y - Title III served
  • N - Not Title III served

eScholar Name: LEP/ELL ELIGIBILITY

96 - TITLE I PART A​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

A Title I Part A indicator to identify students that are Title I Part A participants.

Business Rules:

If your Title I Part A Program is delivered school-wide, ensure that all values are Y.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • Y - Title I Part A
  • N - Not Title I Part A

eScholar Name: PROGRAM SERVICE CODE

97 - GRADE 09 ENTRY DATE​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 10

The school year in which the student entered 9th grade for the first time. The school year is represented by a single date indicating the whole school year; June 30 at the end of the standard school year is used to designate a school year.

Business Rules:

This field is required for any student enrolled in grades 9 – 12.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • 2024-06-30

98 - LEA ENTRY DATE​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 10

The most recent date that the student entered the LEA. If a student withdraws from the LEA then returns, the date of the student's re-enrollment in the LEA.

Business Rules:

This should only be the current school year for new students in their first year at an LEA. LEAs with registered AAPs may code all AAP grade-level students with a July 1 reporting year date (2019-07-01).

eScholar Name: DISTRICT ENTRY DATE

Sample / Valid Value:
  • 2016-07-01

99 - SCHOOL ENTRY DATE​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 10

The most recent date that the student entered the school. If a student withdraws from the LEA then returns, the date of the student's re-enrollment in the LEA. If the student changes schools within the LEA due to a grade change, during the school year, do not update the school entry date.

Business Rules:

For new students in their first year at a school only, this should be the current school year. LEAs with registered AAPs may code all AAP grade-level students with a July 1 reporting year date (2019-07-01).

Sample / Valid Value:
  • 2019-07-01

109 - STATE ENTRY DATE​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 10

The first date that the student entered any public LEA in Pennsylvania (Do not use student's date of birth). If a student left PA public education and then returned to any public LEA in PA, the date of the student's re-enrollment in that LEA.

Business Rules:

This field must be populated with the actual date. LEAs with registered AAPs may code all AAP grade-level students with a July 1 reporting year date (2019-07-01).

Sample / Valid Value:
  • 2016-07-01

110 - DATE FIRST ENROLLED IN A US SCHOOL​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 10

The date that the student first enrolled in a US school. For this purpose, enrollment in a school in Puerto Rico does not count as enrollment in a US school. Use best information available on prior enrollments.

Business Rules:

This data is needed for EL students only.

This field does not apply to AAPs students that are coded as "Current EL" (codes 01 or 06, but not 98 in Field 45) in Field 41 EL STATUS.

eScholar Name: INITIAL US ENTRY DATE

Sample / Valid Value:
  • 2013-08-01

111 - HOMELESS STUDENT​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

(G) Homeless individuals described in section 725 of the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act (42 U.S.C. 11434a) Section 725 of the McKinney-Vento Act, as amended by the ESSA, defines the following terms: (a) Homeless children and youths means individuals who lack a fixed, regular, and adequate nighttime residence. The term includes— (1) Children and youths who are sharing the housing of other persons due to loss of housing, economic hardship, or a similar reason; are living in motels, hotels, trailer parks, or camping grounds due to the lack of alternative adequate accommodations; are living in emergency or transitional shelters; or are abandoned in hospitals. (2) Children and youths who have a primary nighttime residence that is a public or private place not designed for or ordinarily used as a regular sleeping accommodation for human beings. (3) Children and youths who are living in cars, parks, public spaces, abandoned buildings, substandard housing, bus or train stations, or similar settings; and (4) Migratory children (as defined in section 1309 of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965, as amended), who qualify as homeless because they are living in circumstances described in this definition.

Business Rules:

Report Adult Affidavit (AAP) students only.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • Y
  • N

eScholar Name: HOMELESS STATUS CODE

112 - MIGRANT STUDENT​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

Migrant student is defined as a student who is a migrant worker or whose parent or spouse is a migrant worker. U.S. Department of Health and Human Services definition identifies a migrant worker as "an individual whose principal employment is in agriculture on a seasonal basis, who has been so employed within the last 24 months, and who establishes for the purpose of such employment a temporary abode."

Business Rules:

Report Adult Affidavit (AAP) students only.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • Y
  • N

eScholar Name: MIGRANT STATUS CODE

113-116​

Fields not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

117 - DISTRICT OF RESIDENCE AUN​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 9

The AUN of the school district where the parent/legal guardian resides.

Business Rules:

This must be one of the following: one of the 500 Pennsylvania school districts. the generic PA state code of 999999999. The generic out-of-state code of 888888888. Refer to Appendix N for a complete list of Residency Status Codes. This field is checked by the Data Quality Engine (DQE)

Sample / Valid Value:
  • 123456789

eScholar Name: DISTRICT CODE OF RESIDENCE

120 - STUDENT IS A SINGLE PARENT​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

Specifies whether the student is a single parent. A single parent is any individual who is unmarried or legally separated from a spouse and who has a minor child or children for which the parent has either custody or joint custody or is pregnant. This is a Perkins requirement. This is an element required for secondary and adult affidavit program CTE students only.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • Y
  • N

eScholar Name: PARENTAL STATUS CODE

123 - HOME LANGUAGE CODE​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 4

The NCES Code for the native language a child is/was exposed to that resulted in identification as an English Learner. Conditionally required if native language is other than US English.

Business Rules:

LEAs with registered AAPs may leave this field blank for all AAP grade-level students.

Sample / Valid Value:

See Appendix J for a complete list of valid NCES country and language codes.

124​

Field not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

125 - YEARS IN US SCHOOLS​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

An integer value (1, 2, or 3) that indicates the years that a student meets the Title III immigrant definition as follows: A student who: is age 3 – 21; and was not born in any U.S. state; and has not been attending one or more schools in any one or more U.S. state for more than three full academic years. Students from Puerto Rico are not considered Immigrants. Foreign Exchange students should be considered immigrants. This information is used for Title III Immigrant funding purposes only.

Business Rules:

In the case of re-entry into US schools, the calculation for YEARS IN US SCHOOLS is cumulative. Calculation of number of months is from date of enrollment to October 1 of the current school year. Use best information available.

Sample / Valid Value:

1 - 0 – 12 months 2 - 13 – 24 months 3 - 25 – 36 months

126 - NAME SUFFIX​

OptionalUpdateableMax Length: 10

An appendage, if any, used to denote an individual's generation in their family (e.g., Jr, Sr, III).

Business Rules:

Suffix should be split out from last name, both here and in PAsecureID. If student previously submitted into PAsecureID with suffix in last name field, resubmit into PAsecureID with the suffix split out. NOTE: DO NOT create a new PAsecureID; edit the existing one. See the October Student How-To Guide for further information.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • Jr
  • Sr
  • I
  • II
  • III
  • IV
  • V
  • VI
  • VII
  • VIII
  • IX
  • X

127-130​

Fields not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

131 - FOOD PROGRAM ELIGIBILITY​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

The student's eligibility to participate in the free/reduced lunch program.

Business Rules:

All students receiving free or reduced-price lunch in Community Eligibility Provision (CEP) schools are to be reported as food program eligible, regardless of their economic status. Students in non-CEP schools are to be reported as eligible if they are eligible to participate in the free or reduced-price lunch program.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • F - Free
  • R - Reduced price
  • N - Neither

132​

Field not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

133 - LAST NAME​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 60

The legal last name (surname) of the student.

Business Rules:

See the October Student How-To Guide for new guideline regarding hyphenated last names. Do not include suffix in last name.

eScholar Name: LAST NAME LONG

Sample / Valid Value:

Harley

134 - FIRST NAME​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 60

The legal first name of the student.

eScholar Name: FIRST NAME LONG

Sample / Valid Value:

George

135-141​

Fields not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

142 - MIDDLE NAME​

OptionalUpdateableMax Length: 60

The middle name of the student.

Business Rules:

Whenever possible, this is to be the FULL middle name, not just the middle initial.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • Howard

143-153​

Fields not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

154 - ADDRESS 3​

OptionalUpdateableMax Length: 40

Additional information concerning the street address of the student's mailing address, such as a building or site number.

Business Rules:

Address information is needed for CTE secondary students and Perkins funded CTE adult affidavit program students.

155-164​

Fields not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

165 - SCHOOL NUMBER OF RESIDENCE​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 4

The PDE defined, 4-digit code of the public school that the student would attend, based on (1) their grade level and (2) home address of their parents/legal guardian. This must be a School Number related to the reported Field 117 (DISTRICT OF RESIDENCE AUN).

Business Rules:

If the district of residence AUN is the PA State Code 999999999 use School Number 9999. If the district of residence AUN is out of state (88888888) use 9999. If the district of residence does not have a school with the appropriate grade level for the student (a 12th grade student whose district of residence does not have a high school), use 0000. Required for all LEA types EXCEPT Charter Schools. For charter school students (optional) use the School Number of the school the student would attend in the district of residence if not in the charter school. LEAs with registered AAPs may code all AAP grade-level students as 9999.

eScholar Name: LOCATION CODE OF RESIDENCE

Sample / Valid Value:

1234

166 - OUT OF WORKFORCE INDIVIDUAL​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

An indication of whether the student is considered an Out of Workforce Individual (Displaced Homemaker). The term Out of Workforce Individual means an individual who, "(A)(i) has worked primarily without remuneration to care for a home and family, and for that reason has diminished marketable skills; (ii) has been dependent on the income of another family member but is no longer supported by that income; or (iii) is a parent whose youngest dependent child will become ineligible to receive assistance under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 601 et seq.) not later than 2 years after the date on which the parent applies for assistance under such title"; and "(B) is unemployed or underemployed and is experiencing difficulty in obtaining or upgrading employment."

Business Rules:

This field is needed for secondary and adult affidavit CTE students only.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • Y
  • N

167 - SPECIAL EDUCATION REFERRAL (Special Education Services Provided Outside the LEA)​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

Special Education students that are receiving 100% of their services from the following PIMS Reporting agencies (SD, IU, CS, CTC, PRRI, SJCI) instead of their Special Education Reporting Entity (School District or Charter School). If the Special Education Reporting Agency is also the educating LEA, this field must equal N. If the student is referred to a Licensed Private, APS or other similar entities this field must also equal N.

Business Rules:

Must be a Special Education student. Valid Value in Field 38 (SPECIAL EDUCATION) must be Y or E to value this field with a Y.

District of Residence and Charter Schools are the only entities allowed to have Y in this field.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • Y
  • N

eScholar Name: SPED REFERRAL CODE

168-181​

Fields not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

182 - HOME ADDRESS STATE COUNTY CODE​

OptionalUpdateableMax Length: 2

The 2-digit county code of the student's mailing address; counties in PA are numbered 01 – 67 in alphabetical order.

Sample / Valid Value:

See Appendix D for the full list of state abbreviations.

183-188​

Fields not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

189 - FUNDING DISTRICT AUN​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 9

The AUN of the school district that is financially responsible for the student. In most cases, this will be the same district as reported under Field 117 (DISTRICT OF RESIDENCE AUN), EXCEPT in the case of non-resident foster or institutionalized students.

Business Rules:

This must be one of the Pennsylvania school districts. Refer to section "Students to be Reported in PIMS." Also, refer to Appendix N in Volume 2 of the PIMS User Manual for a full list of Residency Status Codes. LEAs with registered AAPs may code all AAP grade-level students with the AUN of the school district where the CTE AAP education entity is located.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • 123456789

eScholar Name: FUNDING DISTRICT CODE

190 - CTE INDICATOR​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

This field is only required for students educated by LEA schools classified as either "occupational" or "comprehensive" Career and Technical Centers (CTCs) on PDE's EdNA website This field indicates whether or not a normal full complement of "primary academics" is administered and provided to this student by the reported CTC school identified in Field 2 (SCHOOL NUMBER) of this template. For this data element, "primary academics" include English, Reading/Language Arts, Language Instruction Education Program (LIEP), Mathematics, Sciences, and Social Studies (history, economics, geography, and civics and government). Note: According to the Attribution Map used for assessment and accountability, if an assessed subject is taught, the occupational or comprehensive CTC will be attributed with the student's participation and performance.

Business Rules:

This field must be populated for all students educated by occupational and comprehensive CTC schools as identified in Field 2 (SCHOOL NUMBER) of this template.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • Y
  • N

191-206​

Fields not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

207 - MILITARY FAMILY CODE​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

Indicate whether the student's parent/guardian is an active duty member of a branch of the United States Armed Forces (Army, Navy, Air Force, Marine Corp, and Coast Guard) including full-time National Guard.

Business Rules:

Set field to "Y" if the condition is true at any time during the school year, otherwise set to "N". Parent/Guardian includes legal guardian or other person standing in loco parentis (such as grandparent or stepparent with who the child lives, or a person whom is legally responsible for the child's welfare including a foster parent on active military duty. It is not applicable for group home, detention centers and wards of state.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • Y
  • N

208​

Field not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

209 - FOSTER STUDENT​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

An AAP student who is in or has aged out of Foster Care.

Business Rules:

Report Adult Affidavit (AAP) students only.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • Y
  • N

eScholar Name: FOSTER CARE INDICATOR

210-211​

Fields not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

212 - PSSA / PASA ASSESSMENT​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

Identifies the associated assessment(s) in which a student participates throughout the given school year. This field will facilitate PDE parsing data for LEAs administering the test and support the creation of pre-code labels by various testing vendors. Follow these rules for applying the assessment participation code: • LEAs with PASA eligible students in grades 3-8 and 11 use Code A regardless of testing agency. (Do not use code I) • LEAs not administering an assessment to a student are to use Code I. • LEAs administering an assessment indicate the appropriate code. NOTE: Purposes of this field are to generate precode labels for the PSSA and to produce the PIMS PASA DLM Enrollment Report. This field is not used for PSSA Accountability. During the Accountability snapshot, this field can remain unchanged. Students in grades 3-8 must test in the PSSA (code B) or PASA (code A) per Chapter 4 requirements. The LEA should use Code I (Not Participating) for the student that is dually enrolled in a PIMS-reporting LEA, but being educated outside the reporting LEA.

Business Rules:

If PASA Testing Agency AUN (field 226) is reported, this must be A.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • A - PASA, no PSSA precode label
  • B - PSSA, receive precode label
  • I - Not participating in any of these assessments
  • N - PSSA Online testing, no label required

eScholar Name: ASSESSMENT PARTICIPATION CODE

213​

Field not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

214 - KEYSTONE WINTER ASSESSMENT​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 20

Identifies that a student will participate in a Winter Keystone Assessment. This field will facilitate PDE parsing data for LEAs administering the test and support the creation of pre-code labels by various testing vendors. NOTE: Adherence to these rules will reduce the need for hand-bubbling of test booklets.

Business Rules:

NOTE: The purpose of this field is to generate precode labels. This field is not used for Accountability.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • Y - Receive precode label
  • N - Not testing
  • O - Online testing, no label required

eScholar Name: ASSESSMENT PARTICIPATION CODE 2

215 - KEYSTONE SPRING ASSESSMENT​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 20

Identifies that a student will participate in a Spring Keystone Assessment. This field will facilitate PDE parsing data for LEAs administering the test and support the creation of pre-code labels by various testing vendors. NOTE: Adherence to these rules will reduce the need for hand-bubbling of test booklets.

Business Rules:

NOTE: The purpose of this field is to generate precode labels. This field is not used for Accountability.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • Y - Receive precode label
  • N - Not testing
  • O - Online testing, no label required

eScholar Name: ASSESSMENT PARTICIPATION CODE 3

216 - KEYSTONE SUMMER ASSESSMENT​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 20

Identifies that a student will participate in a Summer Keystone Assessment. This field will facilitate PDE parsing data for LEAs administering the test and support the creation of pre-code labels by various testing vendors. NOTE: Adherence to these rules will reduce the need for hand-bubbling of test booklets.

Business Rules:

NOTE: The purpose of this field is to generate precode labels. This field is not used for Accountability.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • Y - Receive precode label
  • N - Not testing
  • O - Online testing, no label required

eScholar Name: ASSESSMENT PARTICIPATION CODE 4

217 - AUN OF ENROLLMENT​

RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 9

Provide the AUN of the LEA providing the majority of the student's core academic education. Only PIMS Reporting Agency AUNs are valid (SD, IU, CS, CTC, APS, PRRI, SJCI). If the AUN is not PIMS-reporting, this should equal Field 1. Informs various indicators in the Future Ready PA Index.

Business Rules:

This field is used to identify the LEA providing the majority of the student's core academic education. If this is the same as the reporting LEA (SUBMITTING AUN is reported in Field 1), a school enrollment must exist in the warehouse, or in an accompanying school enrollment upload file.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • 123456789

eScholar Name: DISTRICT OF ENROLLMENT CODE

218-220​

Fields not collected. Must be delimited in template file.

221 - ACCESS FOR ELLS ASSESSMENT​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 20

Identifies that a student will participate in an EL Assessment. This field will facilitate PDE parsing data for LEAs administering the test and support the creation of precode labels by various testing vendors. NOTE: Adherence to these rules will reduce the need for hand-bubbling of test booklets.

Business Rules:

This data is needed for EL students only.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • E - ACCESS for ELLs precode label
  • A - Alternate ACCESS for ELLs precode label
  • O - Other LEA requesting precode label

eScholar Name: ASSESSMENT PARTICIPATION CODE 5

222 - ELA ASSESSMENT EXEMPTION INDICATOR​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 1

Student will have been enrolled in US school < or equal to 12 months by the end of current year ELA/Keystone Literature testing window and was not previously exempted.

Business Rules:

A student can only be assigned a Valid Value of Y once in their educational career.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • Y
  • N

223 - AUN OF SENDING CHARTER SCHOOL​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 9

The 9-digit AUN of the charter school that contracted with the educating LEA to educate a charter school student.

Sample / Valid Value:

123456789

224 - SENDING CHARTER SCHOOL NUMBER​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 4

The PDE defined 4-digit code of the charter school that contracted with the educating LEA to educate a charter school student.

Sample / Valid Value:

1234

225 - GENDER IDENTITY​

OptionalUpdateableMax Length: 1

Identifies a student's personal conception of oneself. This is for state and local use.

Sample / Valid Value:

X - Gender Non-binary or Not Listed (identify exclusively as neither male nor female)

226 - PASA TESTING AGENCY AUN​

Conditionally RequiredUpdateableMax Length: 9

AUN of the agency administering the PASA for the student. This field places a student on the PIMS PASA DLM Enrollment Report of the agency that will assess the student using PASA DLM.

Business Rules:

Must be valid in EdNA. Required if PSSA/PASA Assessment (field 212)= A.

Sample / Valid Value:
  • 123456789

eScholar Name: TESTING AGENCY

Rules​

See the Rules Section of the Template Domain

Edit this page

Student Domain
Next

    • 41 - EL STATUS
    • 42-44
    • 45 - LIEP TYPE (Language Instruction Educational Program)

    • 52-64

    • 73 - FOREIGN EXCHANGE STUDENT
    • 80 - GIFTED
    • 88 - ECONOMIC DISADVANTAGED STATUS CODE

    • 90 - MOBILITY CODE
    • 91 - DIPLOMA TYPE CODE
    • 92
    • 93 - ALTERNATE STUDENT ID
    • 94
    • 95 - TITLE III EL ELIGIBILITY
    • 96 - TITLE I PART A
    • 97 - GRADE 09 ENTRY DATE
    • 98 - LEA ENTRY DATE
    • 99 - SCHOOL ENTRY DATE
    • 109 - STATE ENTRY DATE
    • 110 - DATE FIRST ENROLLED IN A US SCHOOL
    • 111 - HOMELESS STUDENT
    • 112 - MIGRANT STUDENT
    • 113-116
    • 117 - DISTRICT OF RESIDENCE AUN
    • 120 - STUDENT IS A SINGLE PARENT
    • 123 - HOME LANGUAGE CODE
    • 124
    • 125 - YEARS IN US SCHOOLS
    • 126 - NAME SUFFIX
    • 127-130
    • 131 - FOOD PROGRAM ELIGIBILITY
    • 132
    • 133 - LAST NAME
    • 134 - FIRST NAME
    • 135-141
    • 142 - MIDDLE NAME
    • 143-153
    • 154 - ADDRESS 3
    • 155-164
    • 165 - SCHOOL NUMBER OF RESIDENCE
    • 166 - OUT OF WORKFORCE INDIVIDUAL
    • 167 - SPECIAL EDUCATION REFERRAL (Special Education Services Provided Outside the LEA)
    • 168-181
    • 182 - HOME ADDRESS STATE COUNTY CODE
    • 183-188
    • 189 - FUNDING DISTRICT AUN
    • 190 - CTE INDICATOR
    • 191-206
    • 207 - MILITARY FAMILY CODE
    • 208
    • 209 - FOSTER STUDENT

    • 216 - KEYSTONE SUMMER ASSESSMENT

    • 224 - SENDING CHARTER SCHOOL NUMBER
